relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: A peace/well-being offering may come from herd, male or female, complete before YHWH.
The hand rests on the offering, and blood/life circles the altar.
The inner fat is brought near as fire-offering.
The inward portions are lifted for YHWH.
The offering rises as soothing scent upon the altar.
A flock offering of peace may be male or female.
A lamb may be brought near before YHWH.
The hand rests, the offering is made, and blood/life surrounds the altar.
The lamb’s rich portions are brought near.
The inner portions are removed for the altar.
The priest turns them to smoke as food of fire-offering.
A goat too may be brought near.
The hand rests, and blood/life is thrown around the altar.
The goat’s inner fat is brought near.
Blood/life and fat are not eaten; life and abundance are reserved as holy.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 3 gives the offering of wholeness: peace is brought near through shared life-symbols, abundance reserved to YHWH, and the altar receiving the scent of restored communion.
